data.content: The file is generated successfully (we can see the message), but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it. The fix is to include the generated file in `Compile` within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild `Compile` items are already collected before `CoreCompile` runs, so the generated file exists on disk but isn't included in the compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the `GenerateSampleCode` target using an `ItemGroup` with a `Compile Include`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />


</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added inside the target, after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->

    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. The issue was that the `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild collects `Compile` items before `CoreCompile` runs, so the generated file was invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
